full_content:
All cross-cutting technical requirements MUST be mechanically enforced for all implementation proposals, via two-layer defense in depth (write-time + review-time)

Every cross-cutting technical requirement (a rule that constrains multiple implementation surfaces — e.g., project-root-boundary, canonical-terminology, file-bridge-protocol Specification Linkage Gate, etc.) MUST be enforced mechanically at TWO layers, in defense-in-depth fashion:

LAYER 1 — Write-time PreToolUse block (fast feedback):
- The formal-artifact-approval-gate.py PreToolUse hook (or a peer hook) MUST consult the cross-cutting-requirements registry on every Write/Edit operation against bridge proposal files (`bridge/*.md`), and on every Write/Edit/Bash operation that creates or mutates files matching the registered requirements' file_scope patterns.
- For each registered requirement, the hook MUST run that requirement's fast_check (a regex pattern, glob match, AST query, or scripted command) against the operation's payload (proposal text, file content, or command).
- If any fast_check reports a violation, the hook MUST hard-block the operation with an error message naming: the violated requirement ID, the rule text, the source DELIB, and the specific evidence of violation.
- The hook MUST NOT permit operation to proceed without revision OR an owner-authorized exception captured as a sub-DELIB.

LAYER 2 — Codex review-time block (slow but thorough):
- The Codex Loyal Opposition review skill MUST load the cross-cutting-requirements registry at the start of every NEW or REVISED bridge entry review.
- For each registered requirement, Codex MUST run that requirement's deep_check (a broader text analysis, KB query, cross-reference validation, or contextual judgment procedure) against the bridge proposal text + the cited code paths + the relevant project state.
- If any deep_check reports a violation, Codex MUST issue NO-GO with the violation as a numbered finding (per .claude/rules/loyal-opposition.md finding standard: claim + evidence + severity + impact + recommendation).
- Codex MUST NOT issue GO on a proposal that violates any registered cross-cutting requirement, except when the proposal explicitly cites an owner-approved exception DELIB and the exception's scope covers the proposal.

REGISTRY CONTRACT (specified in DCL-CROSS-CUTTING-REQUIREMENTS-REGISTRY-001):
- Cross-cutting requirements live in a single canonical registry whose schema, location, and update procedure are governed by the companion DCL.
- Each registered requirement carries: id, title, rule text (verbatim from source rule file), source DELIB / GOV / ADR / DCL ID, file_scope (glob patterns this rule applies to), fast_check, deep_check, exceptions list (each with its authorizing DELIB ID and scope).

INITIAL POPULATION CHARTER:
The registry MUST be initially populated from these existing rule files:
- CLAUDE.md "Mandatory Project Root Boundary" section
- .claude/rules/project-root-boundary.md
- .claude/rules/canonical-terminology.md
- .claude/rules/file-bridge-protocol.md (Mandatory Specification Linkage Gate, Mandatory Specification-Derived Verification Gate, Mandatory Project Root Boundary Gate)
- .claude/rules/codex-review-gate.md
- .claude/rules/loyal-opposition.md File Safety Rule
- .claude/rules/operating-model.md §1 (interrogative default for owner factual claims) and §2 (canonical terminology consistency)
- DCL-ARTIFACT-APPROVAL-HOOK-001 (formal-artifact-approval gate behavior)

Each MUST get a fast_check + deep_check definition before this GOV's status promotes from `specified` to `verified`.

ENFORCEMENT WAIVER PROCEDURE:
- A cross-cutting requirement may be temporarily relaxed for a specific proposal ONLY via an owner-approved waiver DELIB carrying: DELIB ID, scope (the exact proposal IDs covered), expiry (specific bridge cycle / release version / date), residual risk.
- Waivers MUST be cited explicitly in the proposal's Specification Links section and in the Codex review record.
- Per the file-bridge-protocol.md F3 schema (Slice 8.6 -003), the same waiver discipline applies here.

Rationale:
The Slice 8.6 incident (S330, 2026-05-04) demonstrated the failure mode of voluntary-compliance enforcement: the project-root-boundary rule existed in CLAUDE.md and .claude/rules/project-root-boundary.md, was loaded at session start, and was visible to Prime Builder. Prime Builder still violated it across 12 commits because no mechanical layer blocked the violation. Codex review didn't catch it either because the rule was not in Codex's review checklist. Multi-layer mechanical enforcement closes both gaps: write-time catches obvious violations instantly; review-time catches subtle ones.

Owner approval: Approve as drafted (S330 chat, 2026-05-04, via AskUserQuestion). Per owner clarification: Option C (defense in depth, both layers).
explicit_change_request: Owner approved insertion via AskUserQuestion: "Approve as drafted". This artifact spawns from DELIB-S330-CROSS-CUTTING-REQUIREMENTS-MECHANICAL-ENFORCEMENT. Source intent: All cross-cutting technical requirements specifications must be tracked and mechanically enforced (or as close as we can get) for all implementation proposals.
change_reason: S330 spawn-queue item: GOV-CROSS-CUTTING-REQUIREMENTS-MECHANICAL-ENFORCEMENT-001 created from DELIB-S330-CROSS-CUTTING-REQUIREMENTS-MECHANICAL-ENFORCEMENT per just-approved DELIB-S330-SPEC-CAPTURE-TRANSPARENCY transparency rule.
